name: Ramesh Mistry
accuracy_speed_tradeoff: Speed is critical – minimal latency overhead
pain_point: We process over 50 lakh transactions daily with a team of 4 fraud analysts. Case volume is completely unmanageable without automated prioritisation.
additional_requirements: Batch scoring API for overnight risk rescoring of the full customer portfolio — not just real-time transactions.
comments: Would appreciate a mobile-responsive version of the analytics dashboard for on-call fraud analysts.

name: Anurag Mane
pain_point: QR code fraud is increasing sharply. Fake merchants register QR codes under impersonated business names and collect funds for weeks before detection.
additional_requirements: Real-time chargeback dispute integration with Visa/Mastercard dispute resolution APIs.
comments: Would be keen to run a proof-of-concept on our live transaction stream — happy to share anonymised data.

name: Nishant Rajan
accuracy_speed_tradeoff: Accuracy is everything – take as long as needed
pain_point: We have zero explainability in our current model. When auditors ask why a transaction was blocked, we cannot answer — this is a compliance nightmare.
additional_requirements: Aadhaar seeding validation to cross-check beneficiary identity against UIDAI records.
comments: The pre-authorization paradigm is exactly what India's payment ecosystem needs. Our entire industry is still reactive.

name: Arjun Chandra
pain_point: Customer complaints about frozen accounts cost us 2 crore+ annually in relationship management. The legal team is under pressure to reduce preventive blocks.
additional_requirements: Multi-region deployment with active-active failover across Mumbai and Hyderabad AWS regions.

name: Dev Sahoo
pain_point: Multi-language fraud is a blind spot. We receive narrative remarks in Hindi and Marathi in transaction notes but our model only processes English tokens.
additional_requirements: Integration with our existing Finacle CBS ledger system via ISO 8583 messaging.
comments: The regulatory compliance section shows deep understanding of RBI/NPCI requirements. Rare to see in a product this early.

name: Madhav Gokhale
pain_point: Mule accounts are our primary threat vector. We see dormant accounts activated, credited with large sums, and fully withdrawn within 48 hours.
additional_requirements: Automated STR (Suspicious Transaction Report) generation for FIU-IND in prescribed XML schema.
comments: UPI fraud is an arms race — the threat intelligence update cadence (weekly) is critical to stay ahead.

name: Ashok Rao
pain_point: We onboarded a third-party fraud vendor 2 years ago but their model was trained on US data. India-specific patterns like digital arrest and UPI phishing are completely missed.
additional_requirements: Automated STR (Suspicious Transaction Report) generation for FIU-IND in prescribed XML schema.
comments: The pre-authorization paradigm is exactly what India's payment ecosystem needs. Our entire industry is still reactive.

name: Rohit Mane
accuracy_speed_tradeoff: Accuracy is everything – take as long as needed
pain_point: Staff attrition in the fraud team means institutional knowledge about fraud patterns is being lost. We need a system that encodes pattern detection, not analyst intuition.
additional_requirements: Real-time chargeback dispute integration with Visa/Mastercard dispute resolution APIs.
comments: Happy to provide a letter of intent for a pilot deployment — please reach out to our CTO's office.

name: Hiral Naik
accuracy_speed_tradeoff: Accuracy is everything – take as long as needed
pain_point: Graph-based fraud rings are our biggest gap. We know coordinated fraud happens but can't prove it or visualise the network — our current tools are completely flat.
additional_requirements: Custom rule builder UI so our fraud operations team can define and deploy new rules without engineering involvement.
comments: Impressive scope for a prototype. The case management workflow alone is better than what we have in production.

additional_requirements: Multi-region deployment with active-active failover across Mumbai and Hyderabad AWS regions.

name: Sharad Shinde

pain_point: QR code fraud is increasing sharply. Fake merchants register QR codes under impersonated business names and collect funds for weeks before detection.

comments: UPI fraud is an arms race — the threat intelligence update cadence (weekly) is critical to stay ahead.

name: Arjun Joshi
pain_point: We have no velocity tracking across channels. A fraudster can hit our UPI, card, and wallets in parallel and our siloed systems don't see the combined pattern.
additional_requirements: Aadhaar seeding validation to cross-check beneficiary identity against UIDAI records.
comments: The mule account detection via graph centrality is particularly interesting for our PMGSY beneficiary fraud problem.

name: Pravin Trivedi
pain_point: Regulatory reporting is manual and error-prone. Every quarter our compliance team spends 3 weeks manually compiling RBI MIS reports that should be automated.
additional_requirements: A/B testing framework so we can shadow-test new model versions against production without affecting customers.

additional_requirements: Federated learning capability so we can collaborate on fraud pattern detection with partner banks without sharing raw transaction data.

name: Shrey Verma
accuracy_speed_tradeoff: Accuracy is everything – take as long as needed
pain_point: Real-time alerting doesn't exist for us. Fraud is discovered during batch reconciliation, often 6-8 hours after the transactions occurred.
additional_requirements: A/B testing framework so we can shadow-test new model versions against production without affecting customers.
comments: If the p50 latency claim of 5ms is validated in load testing, this is a game-changer for UPI payment flows.

name: Ramesh Tiwari
pain_point: Mule accounts are our primary threat vector. We see dormant accounts activated, credited with large sums, and fully withdrawn within 48 hours.
additional_requirements: Multi-region deployment with active-active failover across Mumbai and Hyderabad AWS regions.
comments: Concerned about model drift — want to understand the automated retraining pipeline in more detail.

name: Ritu Patil
pain_point: Post-transaction detection is useless for UPI — by the time fraud is flagged, the money is already in a mule account. We desperately need pre-authorization capability.
additional_requirements: Per-merchant risk scoring API that our onboarding team can call before approving new merchant registrations.
comments: The pre-authorization paradigm is exactly what India's payment ecosystem needs. Our entire industry is still reactive.

name: Sahil Shinde
accuracy_speed_tradeoff: Accuracy is everything – take as long as needed
pain_point: Staff attrition in the fraud team means institutional knowledge about fraud patterns is being lost. We need a system that encodes pattern detection, not analyst intuition.
additional_requirements: A/B testing framework so we can shadow-test new model versions against production without affecting customers.

name: Aruna Dey
pain_point: Merchant fraud on our platform is invisible to us. We can detect buyer-side fraud but have no risk profiling for the sellers/merchants who register with us.
additional_requirements: Custom rule builder UI so our fraud operations team can define and deploy new rules without engineering involvement.
comments: Real-time WebSocket dashboard would replace three separate tools our team currently uses.
